mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recession periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth during late-cycle expansions reflects capacity utilization tightening and pricing power for manufacturers, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration predicts negative or flat 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns remain positive in over 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal shows no predictive edge when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue streams, which directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals demand destruction and margin compression for cyclical sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines predict positive 5-day returns during tightening cycles, or the signal fails to maintain a negative directional bias when FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0%.
